name: gossip-001-cross-node-propagation
description: An envelope published at node A is observed at node B after a single round of gossip. The envelope's SAID is identical at both observation points; the gossip layer does not modify the wire bytes.

name: gossip-002-said-conflict-rejected
description: Two different envelopes are gossiped under the same SAID. The receiving node MUST reject the second envelope. The SAID is content-addressed (BLAKE3 of the canonical CBOR); two structurally-different envelopes producing the same SAID is either a collision (cryptographically negligible) or an adversarial substitution attempt. Either way, the receiver keeps the first observation and rejects the second.

name: gossip-003-sequence-gap-detection
description: An envelope arrives at node B referencing a previous-history-hash that node B has not seen. Node B MUST detect the gap, request the missing history via gossip backfill, and not commit the envelope's transition until the gap is closed. The expected behaviour is *not* to reject the envelope — it is to defer acceptance pending backfill.
